title: UAE mental health crisis resources
citation: UAE Ministry of Health and Prevention. (2022). Mental Health Atlas: UAE Country Profile. World Health Organization Mental Health Atlas Contributor, Government of the UAE.
content:
If you or someone you know needs urgent mental health support in the UAE, help is available around the clock.

The National Crisis and Emergency Line, operated by the National Programme for Happiness and Wellbeing, can be reached on 920003. This line provides crisis support in Arabic and English and is available twenty-four hours a day, seven days a week. You do not need to be in immediate danger to call. If you are struggling and need to speak to someone, this line is there for you.

SEHA, the Abu Dhabi health system, provides mental health services accessible by calling 800-SEHA, which is 800-7342. Their services include both outpatient mental health care and emergency support for those in acute distress.

The Dubai Health Authority operates mental health services across the emirate. Rashid Hospital in Dubai has an emergency department equipped to receive people experiencing acute psychiatric crises and can provide immediate assessment and care.

If you believe someone is in immediate danger of harming themselves or others, calling 999 for emergency services is always appropriate. Emergency responders are trained to handle mental health situations alongside physical emergencies.

You do not need to wait until a situation becomes an emergency to seek help. If you have been struggling for a period of time, speaking to your primary care doctor is a good starting point. They can refer you to appropriate mental health services and support you in finding the right level of care.

Help is available. You do not have to manage this alone.
